workflow.name: Stress-grid robustness pass on Arm A bidirectional corridor

pre_registration.rationale: The intervention (the --stress flag and the ±5/10/15/20% perturbation grid it activates) is committed at 3d4daf5 BEFORE any measurement at the stress grid is taken. This card is itself sealed in a follow-up draft commit (bdcb85f) and the measurement runs after that. The card_seal_commit points to the procedure-locking commit because the procedure — not the prediction restatement — is what must be unforgeable.

objective.statement: Demonstrate that the Arm A bidirectional corridor structure for SC-CONCEPT-0003 holds under a doubled perturbation magnitude (40 perturbations at ±5/10/15/20% across 5 parameters), not only the original ±5/10% grid (20 perturbations) that scored STABLE 20/20 in robustness_pass_v0.
objective.metric_definition: Count of perturbation conditions in the stress grid whose bidirectional_coverage field is true in the run's summary JSON. The grid is 5 parameters × 8 perturbation steps = 40 conditions.

intervention.description: Run scripts/hyperstition_robustness_pass.py with --stress, which activates the 40-condition stress grid (±5/10/15/20% on each of the 5 perturbable Arm A parameters). All other knobs (grid=81, steps=80, initial-m=0.6, truth/delta range ±0.8, dominance_threshold=0.8) match v0.

denominators.compute: single-process Python; ~30-60s per perturbation on this box, ~20-30 min total wall time for 40+1 runs
denominators.human_effort: ~30 min: seal commit, draft, run, complete, matrix/spine updates
denominators.evidence_cost: low — deterministic numpy + json output, no external dependencies

reversibility.rollback_method: git revert of the --stress flag commit and this card
reversibility.blast_radius: sandy-chaos repo only; no external systems touched

failure_conditions: Any of the 40 stress perturbations collapses bidirectional coverage → claim falsified, decision NOT PASS
Collapse cluster (≥3 collapses) on any one parameter axis → reveals an undisclosed fragility direction, decision NOT PASS
Run output cannot be reproduced from the sealed script + flags → procedure was not actually pre-registered
Card edits after measurement_timestamp change denominators, thresholds, or claim → seal violated; reviewers must reject

measured_outcome.summary: Stress grid (40 perturbations at ±5/10/15/20% across 5 parameters) preserved bidirectional corridor coverage on every condition. Verdict: STABLE 40/40. Corridor intervals matched the v0 baseline within ±1 grid step on the wider perturbations, consistent with the v0 STABLE 20/20 at ±5/10%. No directional asymmetry detected.

decision.rationale: Pre-registered threshold (40 perturbations preserving bidirectional coverage) met exactly. This is an independent prospective card whose intervention surface (the --stress flag and the resulting wider perturbation grid in scripts/hyperstition_robustness_pass.py) does NOT include the leverage harness itself. T-018's self-referential PASS-provisional caveat is therefore retired by this card.
